fields.Name: PheedLoop
fields.Description: PheedLoop event-management connector — syncs attendees, events, sessions, speakers, exhibitors, sponsors, registrations, memberships and 20+ objects via PheedLoop REST API v3 (dual API-key + API-secret headers, org-scoped URL paths).

Configuration.AuthNotes: Dual-header authentication. Every request carries two HTTP headers: X-API-KEY (the API Key) and X-API-SECRET (the API Secret). Neither header is a Bearer token — both are static key/secret strings. The Organization Code is NOT a header; it is embedded as a path segment in every URL: /api/v3/organization/{ORGANIZATION-CODE}/... This is a tri-component scheme: key (header) + secret (header) + org code (URL path). Credentials are found at Dashboard → Settings → Integrations → Rest API → Get Started. API Secret is one-time generated; re-generation invalidates existing integrations. No token expiry or refresh mechanism is documented.

OrganizationCodePlacement.notes: The Organization Code is a non-secret tenant identifier supplied as a URL-path segment, not as a header or query parameter. It must be stored as non-secret configuration on the CompanyIntegration record (e.g. in a Configuration JSON field named OrganizationCode). All 119 collection requests carry this pattern: /api/v3/organization/{ORGANIZATION-CODE}/...

Configuration.APIVersioningNote: Single live API version — v3 — embedded in all URL paths as /api/v3/. No multi-version routing. Current release tagged v3.3.0 in the Postman collection variable `releaseVersion`. The version is returned in all response headers via the `PheedLoop-API-Version` header. No separate version header on requests.

PaginationDefaults.notes: Django REST Framework pagination envelope: {count, next, previous, results}. All list endpoints use page (1-based) + page_size query params. Default page size is 500 for all data endpoints except Reports (max 100). Trailing slash is required on all URLs. Examples from collection: ?page=2&page_size=10 on all list endpoints; ?page=1&page_size=50 on Reports.

IncrementalSyncCapability.notes: Selected objects expose date-range filter query params that enable incremental sync. Not a universal watermark — capability is per-object. Objects with documented incremental filters: Events (date_updated_gte / date_updated_lte, format YYYY-MM-DD), Registrations (date_updated_gte / date_updated_lte), Members (registration_date_updated_gte / registration_date_updated_lte). Ticket Transfer Logs expose dateCreatedAfter / dateCreatedBefore (disabled params in collection, format YYYY-MM-DD). Most other objects (Attendees, Sessions, Speakers, Exhibitors, Sponsors, etc.) have no documented incremental filter — full re-fetch required. The watermark granularity is date (YYYY-MM-DD), not a timestamp.

Configuration.WebhooksNotes: REST Hooks (pheedloop's term for webhooks) are managed via CRUD on /events/{eventCode}/resthooks/. Operations: POST (subscribe), GET (list), GET single, PUT (update), DELETE (unsubscribe). Subscription payload: {action, endpoint}. Observed action types from collection examples: registration.created, attendee.updated. The full set of supported action types is NOT documented in the collection. Hooks are event-scoped (not org-scoped). No signature algorithm or HMAC verification mechanism is documented.
Configuration.BulkOperationsNotes: Bulk endpoints exist for Attendees and Members only. Attendees: POST /attendeesbulk/ (bulk create), PATCH /attendeesbulk/ (bulk update), DELETE /attendeesbulk/ (bulk delete — accepts {codes:[...]}). Members: POST /membersbulk/ (bulk create), PATCH /membersbulk/ (bulk update). Body is an array (not wrapped in a key). These are non-standard parallel paths, not a generic /bulk pattern across all resources.

universalPK.notes: PheedLoop uses string 'code' fields as the primary identifier on most resources (Attendees, Events, Sessions, Speakers, Exhibitors, Sponsors, Exhibitor Booths, Registrations, Ticket Transfer Logs, Surcharges, Announcements, Memberships, Member Organizations, Contact Tags, Speaker Tags, Sponsor Tiers, Exhibitor/Sponsor Promotions). Code pattern: 3-letter prefix + alphanumeric (e.g. ATT..., EVE..., SES..., SPE..., EXH..., SPO..., MBR..., ANN..., HOK...). Exception: Tags uses a numeric 'id' field. REST Hooks use a numeric 'id'. Reports endpoint uses a hardcoded REPORT-CODE path param (not a record PK). The connector must handle the code-vs-id split per IO. Runtime D4 SoftPKClassifier will validate per-IO.

WriteCapability.notes: Full CRUD available for: Attendees (including bulk), Sessions, Session Formats, Exhibitors, Exhibitor Booths, Sponsors, Speakers, Event Announcements, Speaker Tags, Sponsor Tiers, Org Announcements, Memberships, Member Organizations, Contact Tags, REST Hooks. Update-only (no create/delete via API): Events (PATCH only). Read-only (GET only): Tickets, Ticket Transfer Logs, Surcharge, Registration Promotion, Exhibitor Promotion, Sponsor Promotion, Tags, Registrations (list only), Event Attendance (read + check-in/out state transitions). The PATCH verb is used for all update operations except REST Hooks (which uses PUT).

Configuration.ConcurrencyControlNotes: No ETag, If-Match, If-Unmodified-Since, or version-number fields documented in the Postman collection. Only headers present on all requests are X-API-KEY and X-API-SECRET. Conflict resolution must use snapshot-3-way only.
Configuration.DeleteSemanticsNotes: DELETE operations are hard deletes. Descriptions: Sessions DELETE returns 204 on success; Exhibitors/Sponsors DELETE returns 200 with the deleted record's contents; Speakers DELETE returns 200; Speaker Tags/Sponsor Tiers DELETE returns 204. Attendee DELETE 'removes the Attendee from the chosen event' (event-scoped, not org-level deletion). No soft-delete / archive-flag pattern is documented. No tombstone mechanism or deleted-record feed is documented.
Configuration.DefaultSyncDirectionReasoning: Read-only (Pull) by default per policy. Write-path (bidirectional) requires sandbox validation. No ETag/version concurrency control means conflict resolution is snapshot-3-way only.
Configuration.DefaultConflictResolutionReasoning: No ETag or version field — MostRecent is not reliably safe. DestWins is the conservative default when bidirectional is eventually enabled.

ErrorResponseShape.notes: No error response body schema is documented in the Postman collection. Collection contains no response examples (all response bodies are empty in the collection). Error response shape is inferred from Django REST Framework conventions (the framework implied by the DRF pagination envelope): non-2xx responses typically return {detail: string} or field-level validation errors as {field: [error_string]}. This is inferred, not explicitly documented. EvidenceStrength: InferredFromContext — not used for hard constraints.

CustomFieldMarkerPattern.notes: Some resources carry a custom_fields array in their response: [{code, name, type, label, value, options, is_required}]. Observed on Surcharge and Registration Promotion response examples in the Postman collection. This is an embedded array on the parent record, not a separate endpoint. Custom field discovery requires reading the parent resource's custom_fields array at runtime.

OrderSummaries.reason: Sub-endpoints on Exhibitors (/exhibitors/{code}/order/summary/) and Sponsors (/sponsors/{code}/order/summary/) return financial summary data as a sub-resource of the parent, not a standalone collection. Not a syncable record type.

MemberConvertCodes.reason: POST /members/convertcodes/ is a utility operation for code conversion, not a data entity. Out of scope for sync.

MembershipAssign.reason: PATCH /memberships/{code}/assign/ is a special-action endpoint alongside standard PATCH update on the Memberships IO. Handled as part of Memberships IO, not a separate object.

Configuration.TrailingSlashNote: All PheedLoop API URLs must end in a trailing slash. This is stated explicitly in the Postman collection description: 'All URLs should end in a /'.
Configuration.EventScopedPathNote: Event-scoped resources include the eventCode in the path: /api/v3/organization/{ORGANIZATION-CODE}/events/{eventCode}/{resource}/. Organization-scoped resources sit directly under the org root: /api/v3/organization/{ORGANIZATION-CODE}/{resource}/. The connector must maintain an event-code parameter per EntityMap for event-scoped IOs.
Configuration.BiDirectionalPreconditions: Before enabling bidirectional sync: (1) Validate in a PheedLoop sandbox that write operations produce expected results for the target object family. (2) Note that DELETE is hard — orphan removal from PheedLoop is irreversible. (3) No ETag/version control means simultaneous edits in both systems can overwrite each other; DestWins conflict policy is the safe default. (4) Attendee/Member bulk endpoints can be used for efficient batch creates/updates. (5) Some objects are read-only from the API (Tickets, Surcharges, Registrations, Exhibitor/Sponsor Promotions) — these cannot go bidirectional regardless.

fields.Description: Primary key — PheedLoop string 'code' identifier (universal convention).

fields.Name: attendee_organization
fields.Description: Organization code to associate with this membership. Present in Create Membership request body. Source: Postman Create Membership request body.

fields.Name: email
fields.Description: Email of the member being assigned this membership. Source: Postman Create Membership request body.

fields.Name: first_name
fields.Description: First name of the member being assigned this membership. Source: Postman Create Membership request body.

fields.Name: last_name
fields.Description: Last name of the member being assigned this membership. Source: Postman Create Membership request body.

fields.Name: send_confirmation
fields.Description: When true, sends a confirmation email on membership creation. Source: Postman Create Membership request body.

fields.Name: title
fields.Description: Job title of the member being assigned this membership. Source: Postman Create Membership request body.

fields.Name: unassigned
fields.Description: When true, creates an unassigned membership that can be assigned to a member later. Critical create-time control flag. Source: Postman Create Membership request body + description.

fields.Name: message
fields.Description: Response-only field.

fields.Name: EventAttendance
fields.Description: PheedLoop Event Attendance (event-scoped check-in/check-out state). GET .../attendance/ returns checked_in / not_checked_in attendee-code lists; POST .../checkin/ checks attendees in; DELETE .../checkout/ checks them out. Source: Postman collection v3.3.0 folder "Event Attendance".

Configuration.Notes: Asymmetric CRUD: read=GET /attendance/, create(state)=POST /checkin/, delete(state)=DELETE /checkout/, no PATCH update. checkin/checkout bodies are {"codes": [<attendee-code>...]}. attendance state is keyed by attendee code (FK to Attendees).
Configuration.CreateNote: POST /checkin/ is a state mutation returning {attendees,errored_attendees} (arrays of attendee codes), NOT a new record with a retrievable id. CreateIDLocation=n/a so the generic CreateRecord does not scan for an id field. The generic BaseRESTIntegrationConnector path still routes through BuildCreatedResult, which requires a non-empty externalID — a connector-level CreateRecord/ExtractIDFromResponse override (reading attendees[0]) is required for a true Success result. Setting n/a documents the non-standard shape and stops a misleading body-scan for id|ID|externalID.

fields.Name: checked_in
fields.Description: Array of attendee codes currently checked in to the event. Source: GET /attendance/ 200 body.

fields.Name: not_checked_in
fields.Description: Array of attendee codes not yet checked in. Source: GET /attendance/ 200 body.

fields.Name: attendees
fields.Description: Array of successfully checked-in attendee codes (POST /checkin/ response).

fields.Name: errored_attendees
fields.Description: Array of attendee codes that failed check-in (POST /checkin/ response).

fields.Name: Reports
fields.Description: PheedLoop Reports (org-scoped, read-only). GET /reports/{REPORT-CODE}/ returns a paginated DRF envelope {count,next,previous,results}; the result column set is defined by the dashboard-configured report definition (dynamic schema per report code). Operator supplies the report code. Source: Postman collection v3.3.0 folder "Reports".

Configuration.Notes: Dynamic-schema read-only report. The report code (e.g. REP66RIOLKP0ZRV) is a path parameter the operator configures per EntityMap. Result columns vary per report and are discovered at runtime from the `results[]` rows — not statically declarable. Reports max page_size is 100 (lower than the 500 default for data endpoints).
Configuration.PaginationEnvelope: DRF count/next/previous/results — envelope stripped; results[] is the data array with report-code-dependent columns resolved at runtime

fields.Name: results
fields.Description: Dynamic report data array; column schema is determined by the report code (REPORT-CODE path param). Example columns from REP66RIOLKP0ZRV: Date and Time, Name, Email, Title, Category, Payment Status, Last Refund Date, Refund IDs.
